entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The fifth most important cereal crop globally, grown primarily in arid and semi-arid conditions.

entities.name: ARID AND SEMI-ARID CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions where sorghum is primarily grown.

entities.name: SHOOT FLY
entities.description: A pest that causes infestation during early stages of sorghum development.

entities.name: SHOOT FLY INFESTATION
entities.description: A biotic constraint hampering sorghum production during seedling establishment and early growth stages.

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: An abiotic constraint hampering sorghum production during post-flowering growth stages.

entities.name: SHOOT FLY RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to resist shoot fly infestation.

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to withstand terminal drought stress.

entities.name: STAY-GREEN
entities.description: A mechanism associated with terminal drought tolerance.

entities.name: FINE MAPPING
entities.description: The process of narrowing down the location of a QTL on a chromosome.

entities.name: INTROGRESSION-LINE CROSS
entities.description: A method used to develop a fine-mapping population.

entities.name: GENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of determining the genetic makeup of an organism.

entities.name: PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of observing and measuring physical traits.

entities.name: SBI-10L
entities.description: A specific region on sorghum chromosome 10 where shoot fly resistance and stay-green QTLs are mapped.

entities.name: CANDIDATE GENES
entities.description: Genes identified in the target region for functional annotation and characterization.

entities.name: PYRAMIDING
entities.description: The process of combining multiple traits of interest into a single progeny.
